title: Cytisus incarnatus
taxonomy: Cytisus incarnatus is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic pea-like flowers and compound leaves. As a member of the genus Cytisus, it is closely related to other species such as Cytisus scoparius and Cytisus striatus. Within the Fabaceae family, Cytisus incarnatus is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. Further classification within this subfamily is still being researched and may be subject to change.

constituents: Cytisus incarnatus contains alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds as its active constituents.
